channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Charles Barkley calls Canadians 'fools' for breaking Wayne Gretzky's heart over Trump friendship
item.description: Charles Barkley took aim at Canadians who have been critical of NHL legend Wayne Gretzky over his friendship with President Donald Trump, calling them fools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cal women fall to Notre Dame in ACC tournament, but Big Dance awaits Golden Bears
item.description: The Cal Bears put a scare into the No. 6 ranked Irish in the ACC tournament loss and will be back on the court soon for the NCAA Tournament as a likely No. 8 seed.
item.dc:creator: Mercury News staff and wire reports, The Mercury News

item.title: Rantanen dealt to Dallas, lands 8-year contract; Panthers add Marchand at NHL trade deadline
item.description: Mikko Rantanen went back West — but with the Dallas Stars this time following a brief stint in Carolina. And Bruins captain Brad Marchand is leaving Boston after 16 seasons to join the defending Stanley Cup champion Florida Panthers.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Dion Dawkins: Bills 'would've put on a better show' than Chiefs in Super Bowl
item.description: After watching how the Chiefs fared in last month's Super Bowl, Bills tackle Dion Dawkins said his team would've had a "better chance" at beating the Eagles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Panthers add veteran Brad Marchand as they gear up for run at another Stanley Cup title
item.description: The Florida Panthers welcomed the arrival of Boston Bruins captain Brad Marchand in a Friday trade that adds even more grit and experience to the Stanley Cup champions’ roster.
item.dc:creator: Alanis Thames, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Xander Schauffele makes a late push and extends his PGA Tour cut streak to 58 in a row
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — After two months without competition, PGA and British Open champion Xander Schauffele set modest expectations at Bay Hill and achieved one of his goals at the Arnold Palmer Invitational. He gets two more rounds.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Cuban tells Dallas TV station he would have asked for more in return for Doncic
item.description: Former Dallas Mavericks majority owner Mark Cuban says he wouldn’t have traded Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers without receiving much more in return.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Every possible Big Ten Tournament seeding scenario for Michigan basketball
item.description: Michigan basketball only has one game left, but there are still several possible seeding scenarios for the Big Ten Tournament, especially with tiebreakers.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: Lake Placid awaits word on whether it'll be picked as the Olympic sliding site for 2026 Games
item.description: LAKE PLACID, N.Y. (AP) — Picture this: A sliding athlete wins an Olympic medal in Lake Placid next winter, then is chauffeured down to New York for an award ceremony at Rockefeller Center a few days later with thousands of people there to cheer on both moments. It could happen.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shaun White kicks off new league with visions of snowboarding riches on the halfpipe
item.description: ASPEN, Colo. (AP) — Shaun White's next trick might carry more risk than the flips and twists he pulled off for decades inside all those halfpipes he used to dominate. This time, it's about making magic — and money — for the generation of snowboarders who looked up to him for all those years.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daylight saving time is coming and the golf industry can't wait
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Looking forward to more evening sunlight thanks to daylight saving time this weekend? Many in the golf industry like the time change, too, and they are pushing to make that annual switch permanent.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former MLB outfielder Tyler Naquin says his comeback attempt as a pitcher started last year
item.description: GOODYEAR, Ariz. (AP) — As he begins his attempted comeback at a new position, Cleveland Guardians right-hander Tyler Naquin says his transition from playing outfield to pitching was over a year in the making.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bay FC coaching staff facing investigation after complaints over head coach
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Days after Bay FC unveiled renderings for its slick new training facility on Treasure Island, the team is facing more troubling allegations regarding its backroom staff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Knicks guard Jalen Brunson expected to miss time after suffering ankle injury in OT loss to Lakers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — New York star guard Jalen Brunson could miss an extended period of time after suffering an ankle injury during overtime in the Knicks' 113-109 loss to the Los Angeles Lakers on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Francisco State University cutting men's soccer and 2 other sports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- San Francisco State University announced plans on Thursday to cut three sports programs as part of its budget planning. The announced cuts follow a budget meeting last week that was "particularly difficult," according to an open letter posted by SFSU President Lynn Mahoney.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Shorthanded Jazz lose to last place Wizards, 125-122
item.description: WASHINGTON, D.C. (ABC4 Sports) - Kyshawn George scored 23 points, Alex Sarr added 21 and the Washington Wizards edged the Utah Jazz 125-122 on Wednesday night in a battle of two last place teams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lightning acquire Gourde,Bjorkstrand as NHL trade action heats up
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning have answered the cross-state-rival and defending Stanley Cup champion Florida Panthers' first couple of additions ahead of the NHL trade deadline with a big move of their own.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Green Day's Billie Joe Armstrong, rapper Too Short join Oakland Ballers' ownership group
item.description: (KRON) -- East Bay rap icon Too Short and Green Day frontman Billie Joe Armstrong are getting behind the Oakland Ballers. The team announced on Wednesday that the two internationally renowned musicians -- both of whom have East Bay roots -- will be joining the team's ownership group.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Flo Rida set to perform postgame concert at Rate Field this summer
item.description: Flo Rida will perform at a postgame concert at Rate Field following the White Sox game versus the San Francisco Giants on Friday, June 27 at 6:40 p.m. CT, with on-field Postgame Concert passes available to single-game ticket purchasers and White Sox season ticket holders.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: 49ers donate $20K in equipment to 3 Santa Clara high schools
item.description: (KRON) -- Students and athletes at three Santa Clara high schools will be stepping up their game after the San Francisco 49ers recently donated more than $20,000 worth of workout and football training equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: 1A, 2A, 3A, 4A basketball brackets are out, as 5A & 6A Sweet 16s are set
item.description: Postseason basketball has officially ramped up and is in full swing. Here is where every local team stands.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: San Diego FC officials denounce homophobic chant heard at inaugural home match
item.description: "What took place during our first-ever home match does not reflect who we are as a club or the values we stand for," SDFC officials said in a statement Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: First female NBA scout talks trailblazing for Women's History Month
item.description: Laflin was previuosly a cheerleader for three professional sports teams, had a recurring role in "Baywatch" and worked as a model.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pitt football player, HSE grad dies in Fishers crash
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — An 18-year-old football player died in a crash in Fishers late Saturday night. According to a press release sent by the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office Sunday morning, the crash happened around 9:03 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Florida Man Games return for Round 2 as teams vie to be the world’s worst superhero
item.description: The Florida Man Games are back for Round 2, as teams from around the state gather to prove they have what it takes to join the ranks of the world’s worst superhero.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
